content.text:
Advanced Databases
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF11  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Tutorial:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
This course focuses on the essential concepts and technologies for the design, management, and exploitation of modern database systems, covering both theoretical and practical aspects. It allows students to update and deepen their knowledge of databases.
Prerequisites:
Basic database concepts, SQL language, relational algebra.
Course Content (21h)
1. Introduction to Databases
Review of fundamental concepts: relational model, DBMS, query languages
Types of databases: relational, NoSQL, object-oriented, etc.
DBMS architecture: client-server, distributed architectures
Concepts of transaction, concurrency, and data recovery
2. Advanced SQL Programming
SQL review: joins, sub-queries, aggregation functions, views, stored procedures and functions
Advanced SQL: triggers, error handling and management
NoSQL data manipulation languages: JSONiq, Cypher
3. The Object-Relational Model
Presentation of the Object model
Presentation of the Object-Relational model
Object-Relational model concepts (complex types, inheritance...)
Querying Object-Relational databases (SQL3)
4. NoSQL Databases
Introduction to NoSQL databases: types (key-value, document, graph, column)
NoSQL data models: advantages and disadvantages
Case studies: MongoDB, Cassandra, Neo4j
5. Distributed Databases
Distributed systems architecture
Replication and partitioning techniques
Data consistency in a distributed environment: ACID vs BASE
6. Cloud Databases
Service models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Cloud computing and databases
Security considerations in the cloud
7. Database Performance and Security
SQL query optimisation
Indexing and access strategies
Security: access control, data encryption, auditing
Security management in distributed databases
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
Bibliography
R. Elmasri & S. Navathe (2016).
Fundamentals of Database Systems
, 7th Edition, Pearson.
T. Connolly & C. Begg (2014).

, 6th Edition, Pearson.

SQL pour Oracle
. Editions Eyrolles.
Silberschatz, Korth & Sudarshan (2019).
Database System Concepts
, 7th Edition, McGraw-Hill.
Mohamed Fadhel SAAD (2016).
PL/SQL sous Oracle 12c. Guide du développeur
.
R.G.G. Cattell (1994).
Object Data Management
. Addison-Wesley.
P. Selmer (2012).
NOSQL stores and Data analytics tools
. Advances in Data Management.
